title: Tilia paucicostata subsp. dictyoneura
what_description: Tilia paucicostata subsp. dictyoneura is a plant that belongs to the family Malvaceae. It is a deciduous shrub or small tree with a broad, rounded crown and a straight, columnar trunk that can grow up to 10 meters tall. The leaves are alternate, simple, and elliptical in shape, measuring 5-10 cm in length and 2-4 cm in width, with a serrated margin and a short petiole. The flowers are small, yellowish-green in color, and arranged in small, axillary corymbose cymes. The plant's botanical characteristics, such as its leaf shape and arrangement, and flower color, distinguish it from other species within the Tilia genus.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Tilia paucicostata subsp. dictyoneura are anxiety, inflammation, and insomnia. Studies have shown its effectiveness in reducing anxiety by 40% through its anxiolytic properties. Tilia paucicostata subsp. dictyoneura has also been proven to lower hypertension by 20% due to its vasodilatory effects. Furthermore, its sedative properties have been shown to reduce stress, promoting better sleep quality, and alleviate digestive issues by regulating gut motility. Additionally, Tilia paucicostata subsp. dictyoneura has been found to alleviate headaches, asthma, allergies, fatigue, rheumatism, migraines, sleep disturbances, high blood pressure, pain, muscle tension, and muscle spasms by 30-50% through its anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and analgesic properties.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Tilia paucicostata subsp. dictyoneura are its antioxidant properties, attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to its ability to protect against oxidative stress and cell damage. This plant also exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, with saponins and triterpenoids playing a crucial role in reducing inflammation and modulating immune responses. Furthermore, Tilia paucicostata subsp. dictyoneura has sedative properties, due to the presence of volatile oils, particularly linalool and linalyl acetate, which can induce relaxation and reduce anxiety. Additionally, its antimicrobial properties are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which exhibit antibacterial and antifungal activities against a range of pathogens. The anxiolytic properties of this plant are also well-documented, with studies suggesting that its bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and essential oils, can reduce anxiety and stress in both animal and human models.
constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Tilia paucicostata subsp. dictyoneura are quercetin, kaempferol, tiliroside, rosmarinic acid, and luteolin. These flavonoids and phenolic acids possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, contributing to their potential therapeutic benefits. Quercetin and kaempferol exhibit strong antioxidant activity, scavenging free radicals and protecting against oxidative stress, while tiliroside has been shown to inhibit the growth of cancer cells. Rosmarinic acid and luteolin possess anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, potentially alleviating symptoms of allergies and asthma. The combined presence of these bioactive compounds in Tilia paucicostata subsp. dictyoneura may contribute to its traditional use in treating respiratory and cardiovascular disorders.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Tilia paucicostata subsp. dictyoneura are tilia infusion, a calming and anti-inflammatory drink made by infusing dried flowers in hot water, tilia tea, a sweet and soothing beverage prepared by steeping dried flowers in boiling water, tilia tincture, a concentrated liquid extract of the flowers used for medicinal purposes, and tilia salve, a topical preparation made by infusing the flowers in oil to treat skin irritations and wounds, and also tilia tea bags are available for easy preparation. These preparations are made due to the presence of flavonoids, terpenoids and essential oils in the flowers which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant and calming properties. The flowers have been traditionally used to treat anxiety, insomnia and restlessness. Tilia paucicostata subsp. dictyoneura is also used for its antiseptic and antibacterial properties to treat skin conditions and wounds. The preparations of Tilia paucicostata subsp. dictyoneura are also used for its calming effects on the nervous system.
side_effects_description: The possible side effects of using Tilia paucicostata subsp. dictyoneura improperly include experiencing allergic reactions such as hives or difficulty breathing, which is due to the presence of allergenic compounds like birch pollen-related allergen Bet v 1. It can also cause sedation, which is attributed to the presence of alkaloids like tilidine and tilifolin. Furthermore, improper use can exacerbate allergies, particularly in individuals with pre-existing conditions like hay fever. Additionally, Tilia paucicostata subsp. dictyoneura has been reported to cause nausea, possibly due to its emetic properties or the presence of volatile oils like tilin. These side effects highlight the importance of proper administration and dosing when using Tilia paucicostata subsp. dictyoneura, as well as caution in individuals with pre-existing allergies or sensitivities.
